page: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an FPGA Gen2 instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. This configuration provides 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 10.67 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1880 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the f2.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, scoring in the top tier for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Cryptographic operations and ray tracing also rank in the top 25% of tested systems. However, memory bandwidth for both read and write operations falls into the average category. While the instance handles text generation for small language models efficiently, text generation for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, performing in the bottom 10% with 2.34 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the f2.12xlarge offers a high-density resource profile, combining substantial memory capacity with fast local NVMe storage. This makes it a balanced option for memory-intensive workloads without requiring dedicated GPU hardware. The instance is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded decompression tasks, image processing, and FPGA-accelerated development workloads, though it is less optimal for running large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an FPGA Gen2 instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It includes 512 GB of DDR4 memory and 1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU capabilities, particularly in cryptographic and decompression tasks, alongside average memory bandwidth. While highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and image processing, it exhibits poor performance during large-scale LLM text generation. This instance provides a high-density hardware profile suitable for memory-optimized workloads and FPGA development without the need for dedicated GPU accelerators.
og_description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is a Nitro-based FPGA Gen2 instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core CPU performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is a Nitro-based instance with 48 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and storage instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and local NVMe SSDs for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with 512 GB DDR4 RAM and 1880 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
Features 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for memory-heavy databases, image processing, and FPGA tasks
